title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly, a physical therapist, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her patient Giovanni 'Gio' Thompson.
Gio, a former patient at Franciscan Children's, had Morquio syndrome and passed away at age 5.
Donnelly was inspired by Gio's spirit and life lessons, motivating her to participate in the marathon.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: The Bruins lost to the Devils 4-3 in OT, jeopardizing their playoff position in the Eastern Conference.
Jack Hughes of the Devils had three assists, reaching 402 career points in 414 games.
David Pastrnak scored twice for the Bruins, who have 81 points with 15 games remaining.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, of Tyngsborough, is charged with murder after remains found under his garage floor, prosecutors say.
Authorities believe the remains may be Jill Kloppenburg, a 47-year-old woman missing from Lowell since January 2025.
Police discovered the remains after radar scans revealed a large patched area in Sullivan's garage floor.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman, from Houston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her son, Charles, and his doctor.
Charles has Dup15q syndrome, and Dr. Ronald Thibert at MGH has been integral in his treatment for 14 years.
Felterman's marathon run aims to raise funds for Massachusetts General Hospital, highlighting their exceptional care.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers, 35, from Belmont, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his wife, Coney, diagnosed with leukemia in 2024.
Coney Flowers underwent over 70 days of treatment, including a bone-marrow transplant, and is now nearly two years cancer-free.
Ben is raising money for Caring for a Cure, highlighting their support and contributions during Coney's treatment.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Massachusetts General Hospital Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie, 33, from Southampton, runs to honor pediatric cancer patients like Maddie and Ashton with the motto 'I Run For Them.'
Donors can honor individuals affected by cancer, adding them to Labrie's 'IRFT' crew as she collects stories of inspiration.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Noah Kahan: Out of Body' premieres on Netflix on April 13.
Kahan's documentary covers his journey since the 2022 success of 'Stick Season' and his Vermont roots.
Kahan's fourth studio album 'The Great Divide' releases shortly after, on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns as a 'special contributor' to WBZ, resuming political analysis and debate moderation.
Keller's 'Keller At Large' interview show is set to return Sunday at 8:30 a.m. with Gov. Maura Healey.
Keller was laid off from WBZ last fall after Paramount's acquisition by Skydance Media; he joined WBZ in 2005.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider ending biannual clock changes, supported by legislation from State Sen. John F. Keenan.
Informal poll shows 83% of Boston.com readers want to stop changing clocks, though opinions vary on preferred time system.
Concerns include health impacts of clock changes and potential coordination issues with neighboring states if changes occur.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested after driving 118 mph and fleeing police in New Hampshire.
The teenager crashed a 2014 Chrysler 300 into a retaining wall in Stratham after failing to navigate a traffic island.
He faces multiple charges, including felony reckless conduct and aggravated DUI, and was held in Rockingham County Jail.
